english: lce-spyań (lce spyang) Stg., dur-spyań (dur spyang) Cs., *kyi-cáń* (kyi cang) W., jackal. — spyań-dug-pa (spyang dug pa) Cs., spyań-tsér (spyang tser) Med., thistle, or kind of thistle, mentioned as an emetic.

1. sbst. and adj.; spyań-po (spyang po) adj., skill; skilful, clever, Lex., Glr. and elsewh.; prob. = gcań(-po) (gcang (po)), q. v.; sometimes confounded with sbyań-ba (sbyang ba), sbyáńs-pa (sbyangs pa), practiced, expert; rig-pa spyań-bas rtsóm-pa kún-la _jug (rig pa spyang bas rtsom pa kun la 'jug) Lt. the clever man finds his way in every thing; spyań-blén (spyang blen) Cs. the clever man and the dunce: Glr.: spyań blen ma nór-ba čig byed dgos (spyang blen ma nor ba cig byed dgos), prob. to be read _byed ('byed), and to be translated: then it must evidently appear, who is clever and who is stupid — 2. vb. = dpyań-ba (dpyang ba).

resp. for mig (mig), eye; spyan bgrád-pa (spyan bgrad pa), gdáń-ba (gdang ba), to stare Cs.; spyan _gyúr-ba (spyan 'gyur ba) v. spyán-pa (spyan pa); spyan _drén-pa (spyan 'dren pa), rarely dróńs-pa (drongs pa), resp. for _drén-pa ('dren pa), to invite, v. _drén-pa ('dren pa); spyan _phyi-ba (spyan 'phyi ba) to wipe the eyes; spyan btsúm-pa (spyan btsum pa) to shut the eyes Cs. Comp. and deriv. spyan-kyúg (spyan kyug) or kyug (kyug) eye-brow Cs. — spyan-dkyús (spyan dkyus) v. dkyus (dkyus). — spyan-bskyúńs mdzád-pa (spyan bskyungs mdzad pa) to protect, to preserve the eyes Sch. — spyán-sńa (spyan snga) before, with, in presence of a dignitary, spyán-sńai grá-pa-rnams (spyan snga'i gra pa rnams) the scholars standing in presence of his Reverence Cs.; mostly in the termin. case: spyán-sńar (spyan sngar) as adv. and postp., rgyál-poi spyán-sńar khrid-pa (rgyal po'i spyan sngar khrid pa) to lead (another) before the king, frq.; rarely in reference to the first pers.: ńai spyán-sńar _oń (nga'i spyan sngar 'ong) they came to me, before my face (sc. Buddha's) Dzl.; less corr. spyán-sńar mdzés-pai skúd-ris (spyan sngar mdzes pa'i skud ris) Mil. in front (on the fore-part of the shoes) beautifully embroidered figures. — spyán-čan (spyan can) having eyes. — spyan-lčibs (spyan lcibs) eye-lid. — spyan-čáb (spyan cab) tears, _byín-pa ('byin pa) to shed; čór-ba (cor ba) to flow from; also to shed, rgyál-bu spyan-čáb śór-ro (rgyal bu spyan cab shor ro) Pth. the prince shed tears. — spyan-_drén (spyan 'dren) one who invites, one that calls to dinner. — spyán-pa (spyan pa) Cs. 1. eye-witness; 2. commissary; 3. Sch. overseer; spyán-du _gyúr-ba (spyan du 'gyur ba) = dpáń-du _gyúr-ba (dpang du 'gyur ba), to see, to know; spyán-pa byéd-pa (spyan pa byed pa) to watch, guard, keep, protect, inspect Sch.; bá-glań-gi spyán-pa (ba glang gi spyan pa) cow-herd (?) Sch. — spyan-_brás (spyan 'bras) apple of the eye. — spyan-mig-bzáń (spyan mig bzang) the western 'king of ghosts', v. rgyal-čén (rgyal cen) sub rgyál-ba (rgyal ba). — spyan-dmigs (spyan dmigs) Sch.: 'the object of vision; the inclination of the mind'. — spyan-sman (spyan sman) medicine for the eyes. — spyan-rtség (spyan rtseg) the wrinkles of the eye-lids Cs. — spyan-zúr (spyan zur) Sch., corner of the eye. — spyan-gzigs (spyan gzigs), costly offerings dedicated to the gods, Mil.; also applied to presents of food, offered to men, Mil.; _búl-ba ('bul ba) to offer such; also _drén-pa ('dren pa). — spyan-yás (spyan yas), Sch., without eyes, blind. — spyan-rás (spyan ras), Sch. the brightness of the eye, a glance of the eye. — spyan-ras-gzígs (spyan ras gzigs) W.; *čan-re-zíg* (can re zig) Cs.: *čen-re-sig* (cen re sig) or *-si* (-si), Ssk. अवलोकितेश्वर, the other (cf. _jam-dpal ('jam dpal)) of the two great half-divine Bodhisattvas of the northern Buddhists, who more particularly is revered as begetter (not creator), redeemer, and ruler of men, and in the first place of the Tibetans, incarnate as king Sroń-tsan-gám-po (srong tsan gam po), Köpp. II, 22. — spyán-lam-du (spyan lam du) seems to be = drúń-du (drung du), spyán-sńar (spyan sngar), Mil. and elsewh.

I. adj. (synon. kun (kun), also dbyińs (dbyings), opp. to sgos (sgos)) 1. general, relating to all, standing higher than all: *t'im-pon čí* (t'im pon ci), chief prefect, governor general C.; adv. spyi (spyi), spyir(-du) (spyir (du)), less frq. spyi-la (spyi la), spyi-na (spyi na), spyir-gyis (spyir gyis), generally, in general, frq. followed by sgos(-kyis) (sgos (kyis)), kyád-par (kyad par), in particular, singly; also like cum tum in Latin; spyi dań _dir (spyi dang 'dir), generally, and here, in this work, Wdń.; spyii sdom (spyi'i sdom), v. sdom (sdom); — spyii kog ji bźin-du (spyi'i kog ji bzhin du) (?) Sch.: 'according to general custom'. — 2. all, C.; lhá-khań spyii bstan-srúń (lha khang spyi'i bstan srung) Glr. — 3. for spyi-bo (spyi bo), v. below. — spyi-sgra (spyi sgra) Cs., general meaning, more corr. sgra-spyi (sgra spyi), Was. (294), general expression. — spyi-gčér (spyi gcer), spyi-tér (spyi ter) Cs., bald-headed. — spyi-tór (spyi tor) = gtsug-tór (gtsug tor) Lex. spyi-tóg (spyi tog), property of the community, common property; W.: *pí-tog-ne toń* (pi tog ne tong) bestow it out of the common funds! — spyi-gdugs (spyi gdugs), v. sgos (sgos). — spyi-pa (spyi pa), head, chief, leader, superintendent, Sch.;
